rationale: The position at which k can be inserted into a sorted vector to keep it sorted is unique: assuming r1 < r2 forces both k < pre[r1] (from sortedness of post1 at r1, r1+1) and pre[r1] <= pre[r2-1] < k (from sortedness of s and post2), contradicting cmp asymmetry. A helper lemma derives this contradiction using `Seq::insert_ensures` for indexing facts and `K::cmp_properties` for transitivity/asymmetry; the prelude reveals `valid`, gathers the sorted hypotheses inside an `if` that mirrors the ensures antecedent (so r1, r2 are known in [0, pre.len()]), calls the lemma, and lifts r1 == r2 to view equality of the two posts.
